task: Upgrade Lyzane ShoeHero 3D scene from flat/static to cinematic (Spline-quality) motion
context: Existing stack: Three.js 0.185.1 via @react-three/fiber 9.6.1 + @react-three/drei 10.7.7. Current scene uses flat ambient+directional lighting, a rigid Math.sin rotation, no environment/reflections, no grounding shadow, no particles, no camera parallax. This changeset upgrades the scene without replacing the existing .obj model pipeline.

changes.description: Replace rigid sine-wave rotation with a layered, damped rotation so the shoe motion feels organic instead of mechanical
changes.location: Inside the useFrame callback that currently sets rotation.y
changes.notes: delta must come from the useFrame(state, delta) signature; if not already destructured, add it

changes.description: Add environment reflections on the leather material and a soft contact shadow so the shoe reads as placed in a scene rather than floating on a flat gradient
changes.location: Inside the <Canvas> JSX, alongside existing lights
changes.notes: Both components are already available via the installed @react-three/drei package, no new dependency needed

changes.description: Add subtle cursor-driven camera parallax so the scene reacts to mouse movement, matching the interactive feel of Spline embeds
changes.location: New useFrame hook inside the Canvas scene component (can be same component as damped-rotation or a sibling)
changes.notes: state.mouse is normalized [-1, 1] by r3f automatically from pointer position over the canvas, no extra event listeners needed

changes.description: Add bloom on the brass rim light and a vignette to darken the edges — the single biggest contributor to the 'premium/cinematic' look in Spline exports
changes.location: Inside <Canvas>, as a sibling to the scene content, typically last child

changes.description: Add slow-drifting brass-colored dust particles for atmospheric depth behind/around the shoe
changes.location: Inside <Canvas>, as a new sibling component, e.g. <DustParticles />

add_component.name: DustParticles
implementation_notes: Randomize initial position within a bounding box roughly matching the shoe's scene bounds (approx [-2,2] x, [0,3] y, [-2,2] z)
In useFrame, increment each point's y position slowly (e.g. delta * 0.05) and wrap back to y=0 when it exceeds y=3
Color: brass (#b5985a), size ~0.02, transparent, opacity ~0.4, depthWrite false

changes.description: Verify canRun3D() and the reduced-motion fallback still apply — none of the above changes should run when canRun3D() returns false
changes.location: Existing canRun3D() gate around the Canvas render
changes.action: no code change required, only a regression check after implementing the above
changes.notes: Confirm ContactShadows, Environment, and postprocessing are not mounted at all in the fallback branch, since they are the most GPU-intensive additions

verification_steps: Run npm install for the two new packages before editing ShoeHero.tsx
Visually confirm the shoe rotation no longer looks like a rigid metronome swing
Confirm reflections are visible on the leather material from the Environment preset
Confirm a soft shadow appears beneath the shoe from ContactShadows
Move the mouse over the hero section and confirm the camera subtly shifts (parallax)
Confirm bloom is visible around bright highlights without over-blowing the whole scene (tune luminanceThreshold if too bright)
Confirm particles drift upward slowly and loop without popping/flickering
Test on a throttled/low-end device profile to confirm the reduced-motion/fallback path skips all of the above and still performs acceptably
